caption: The video showcases a close-up view of a tablet displaying an application called "Alpha Szenszor eNose v1.0.14." The screen features a graph at the top, which appears to be tracking current (in microamperes) over time (in seconds), with multiple lines representing different channels. Below the graph, there is a list of channels numbered from 1 to 22, each associated with a specific color and line style. To the right of each channel, there are toggle switches, some of which are turned on, indicated by their orange color. The interface also includes a "STOP" button at the top right corner. The background suggests that the tablet is being held in a casual setting, possibly during a presentation or demonstration, as part of a table and some blurred objects can be seen. The video likely focuses on explaining or demonstrating the functionality of this electronic nose (eNose) application.
